references.text: Cuenoud, P, MA Del Pero Martinez, P-A Loizeau, R Spichiger, S Andrews & J-F Manen. 2000. Molecular phylogeny and biogeography of the genus Ilex L. (Aquifoliaceae). Annals of Botany 85: 111-122.

Manen, JF, MC Boulter & Y Naciri-Graven. 2002. The complex history of the genus Ilex L. (Aquifoliaceae): evidence from the comparison of plastid and nuclear DNA sequences and from fossil data. Plant Systematics and Evolution 235: 79-98

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 3 June 2026

species.citation: Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1666 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 476; Type: Elmer 11348, Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, Todaya, in the elfinwoods of Mt Calelan at 8000ft.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. DINAGAT (photos), MINDANAO: Davao, ?Davao Oriental (photos).
species.notes: Perhaps not distinct from Ilex microthyrsa Loesen. ex Elmer. Dense thickets on high exposed ridges. 2438m.

species.citation: (Hook. & Arn.) Champ. ex Benth. , Hooker's J. Bot. Kew Gard. Misc. 4 (1852) 329 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 477; ≡ Prinos asprellus Hook. & Arn. , Bot. Beechey Voy. (1833) 176. = Ilex merrillii Briq. , Annuaire Conserv. Jard. Bot. Genève 20 (1919) 421. = Ilex gracilipes Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 237 ; Merrill & Merritt, PJS 5c (1910) 358; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664. var. asprella. Distribution: China, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, Mountain Province, Rizal, Zambales, MINDORO. Notes: Exposed ridges and ravines. 1000-2000m. Native. var. megalophylla T.R.Dudley, Feddes Repert. 91 (1980) 577. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. NEGROS. Native.
species.distribution: China, Philippines, Taiwan. BATAN, BILIRAN, LUZON: Abra, Apayao (photos), Aurora (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya (photos), Rizal, Zambales, MINDORO, NEGROS.

species.citation: Miq. , Verslagen Meded. Afd. Natuurk. Kon. Akad. Wetensch., 2nd Ser. 2 (1868) 84. var. rolfei (Elmer) Loes. ex Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 477; ≡ Ilex rolfei Elmer, LPB 1 (1908) 314. ≠ Ilex formosana auct. non Maxim.; C.B.Rob., PJS 3 c (1908) Bot. 201. Distribution: China, Japan, Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PANAY (photos). Notes: Ravines and on exposed forested ridges. 1400-1800m. Native.
species.distribution: China, Japan, Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PANAY (photos).

species.citation: Thunb. , Fl. Jap. (Thunberg) (1784) 78. = Ilex crenata forma luzonica (Rolfe) Loes. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 78 (1901) 201 ; Merrill & Merritt, PJS 5c (1910) 358; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 477; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664; ≡ Ilex luzonica Rolfe , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 21 (1884) 309. = Eurya myrtilloides Elmer , LPB 1 (1908) 323.
species.distribution: China, India, Japan, Korea, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Taiwan, Tibet, Vietnam. CAMIGUIN, DINAGAT (photos), LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Camarines, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS.
species.notes: On exposed ridges, on the higher mountains often forming dense thickets. 1700-2700m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 273 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; Type: FB 18069 Curran, Merritt & Zschokke (US, iso), Luzon: Benguet, Mt Pulag, slopes in the mossy forest, c. 2500m, 6-Jan-1908.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826-1827) 1149 ; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; Andrews, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 4 (2002) 12; Type: from Java (lecto). = Ilex philippinensis Rolfe , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 21 (1884) 308. = Ilex cymosa var. cumingiana (Rolfe) Loes. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 78 (1901) 76 ; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; ≡ Ilex cumingiana Rolfe , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 21 (1884) 308.
species.distribution: Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BUCAS GRANDE, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Aurora, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Norte, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), SIBUYAN, SICOGON (photos).
species.notes: Locally common in lowland primary forests. Also in peat swamps. (?-)50-800m.

species.citation: Maxim. , Mém. Acad. Imp. Sci. St.-Pétersbourg, 7th Sér. 29 (1881) 46. = Ilex loheri Merr. , PJS 27 (1925) 31 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 252; Type: Loher 14144 (A, iso), Luzon: Rizal, Bantol, Feb-1914.

Elmer ex Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 316, as 'guerreroii' ; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1663, nom. inval., nom. nud.; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; Type: Guerrero s.n., Luzon: Rizal, mountains back of San Mateo, 1910.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Rizal.

species.citation: Maxim. , Mém. Acad. Imp. Sci. St.-Pétersbourg, 7th Sér. 29 (1881) 33. = Ilex fletcheri Merr. , PJS 2c (1907) 279 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1663.
species.distribution: China, Philippines. LUZON: Aurora, Benguet, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, MINDORO.

species.citation: Hayata , J. Coll. Sci. Imp. Univ. Tokyo 30(1) (1911) 55 ; Hu, J. Arn. Arb. 34 (1954) 143, excl. syn. Ilex poneantha Koidz; Hatusima, Mem. Fac. Agr. Kagoshima Univ. 5 (1966) 37.

Loes. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 78 (1901) 432 ; Andrews, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 4 (2002) 18; Type: from Sarawak (lecto). = Ilex racemifera Loes. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 89 (1908) 290 ; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Type: Warburg 14640, Mindanao: Davao. = Ilex foxworthyi Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 193 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1663. = Ilex halconensis (Merr.) Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 358 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 478; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1663; ≡ Embelia halconensis Merr. , PJS 2c (1907) 297. = Ilex apoensis Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1667 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 476; Type: Elmer 11449 (for flower, BISH, US) and 11408 (for fruit, BISH, NA, NY, US), Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, Todaya, upper forested region, 7000ft, Aug-1909. = Ilex pulogensis Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 358 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1664; Type: FB 18145 Curran, Merritt & Zschokke (A, type fragment), Luzon: Benguet, Mt Pulag, mossy forest above 2250m. = Ilex brunnea Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 318 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 477; Type: BS 19461 Ramos (US, iso), Luzon: Quezon, Mt Pular, apparently on trees at the summit of the mountain, 15-Jan-1913.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. BILIRAN, BOHOL, CAMIGUIN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Apayao, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya (photos), Pampanga, Quezon, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato (photos), Davao, Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Usually in mid-high montane forests and exposed ridges. Also recorded as epiphytic or pseudoepiphytic. 300-2300m.

species.citation: Loes. ex Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1663, nom. inval., nom. nud. ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Zambales, MINDANAO: Misamis Occidental.
species.notes: Apparently, no published description exist; certainly none in Elmer’s Leaflets. Exposed ridges. 1800-2400m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 318 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Type: BS 9400 (NY, US, iso), Luzon: Quezon, Mt Binuang, mossy forest, c. 980m, 28-Aug-1909.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Camarines Sur, Quezon, Sorsogon, PALAWAN.

species.citation: Loes. ex Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1664, nom. inval., nom. nud. ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Type: BS 555 Foxworthy, Palawan: Puerto Princesa, Mt Pulgar (Thumb Peak).
species.notes: Apparently, no published description exist; certainly none in Elmer’s Leaflets. Dense thickets on exposed ridges. 1200m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 274 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Type: BS 26396 Ramos & Edaño (A, lecto; US, isolecto), Luzon: Nueva Ecija, Mt Umingan, forest at the summit, probably c. 1000m, 19-Aug-1916.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Nueva Ecija.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 26 (1925) 468 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 249; Type: BS 40311 Ramos & Edaño, Luzon: Benguet, Mt Baudan, mossy forest, c. 2100m, Sep-1921.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet.

species.citation: Merr. ex Elmer , PJS 10c (1915) 317 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1663; Type: Guerrero s.n., Luzon: Rizal, mountains back of San Mateo, 1910.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Rizal.

species.citation: Loes. , Nova Acta Acad. Caes. Leop.-Carol. German. Nat. Cur. 78 (1901) 326. var. benguetensis (Elmer), ined.; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; ≡ Ilex benguetensis Elmer, LPB 5 (1913) 1665; Type: Elmer 8522 (A, iso), Luzon: Benguet, Baguio, along Ipit River, 4000ft, Mar-1907. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet. Notes: P.B. Pelser (4-May-22): It seems from Merrill (1923) that var. benguetensis was never described. I am not sure if it should be recognized as a variety. Forests. 0-1100m. Native.
species.distribution: Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, ?Nueva Vizcaya (photos), Quezon, MINDORO.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 8c (1913) 382 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 479; Type: Wenzel 99 (US, iso), Leyte: Leyte, Dagami, epiphytic on large trees in forest, c. 60m, 5-Jun-1913. = Ilex epiphytica Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1667, nom. illeg., non King; Type: Elmer 13718 (PNH, holo, lost; BISH, MO, NA, NY, US, iso), Mindanao: Agusan del Norte, Cabadbaran, Mt Urdaneta, upon moss and vine-covered large limbs of massive trees in a forested depression at 3500ft between Duros and Cawilanan peaks, Sep-1912.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LEYTE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, SAMAR.
